title: Docker Architecture
description:
## Who Does the Work?

When you type `docker run nginx` in your terminal, which component is actually responsible for pulling the image, creating the container, and managing its lifecycle?

options.text: The `docker` CLI — it does everything locally and only contacts a registry to download images
The Docker **daemon** (`dockerd`) — the CLI sends the request to it over a socket, and the daemon builds, runs and manages containers
The container itself bootstraps and pulls its own image
Docker Hub runs the container remotely and streams it back

explanation: Docker uses a **client–server** model. The `docker` CLI is a thin client that sends commands (over `/var/run/docker.sock` or a TCP socket) to the **Docker daemon** `dockerd`. The daemon does the real work: pulling images from a registry, creating/starting/stopping containers, and managing images, volumes and networks. A registry like Docker Hub only stores and serves images — it never runs your containers.

hints.title: Check what the daemon reports
hints.body: `docker info` queries the daemon and prints server-side details — proof that a separate server process is handling the request.
